original: WCTV TV channel 6 in Tallahassee-Thomasville, Florida (CBS) begins
HMS Beagle, withCharles Darwinon board, reaches the Galápagos Islands
Scottish bacteriologistAlexander Flemingdiscovers penicillin while sorting through petri dishes containing colonies of the bacteria Staphylococcus
Secretariatwins Marlboro Cup in world record 1:45 2/5 for 1¼ miles
John Cobb sets world auto speed record at 350.2 MPH (lasts 1 day)
Boxing's NBA adopts 10-pt-must-scoring-system (10 pts to round winner)
Bill Pinkney, American National Sailing HOF yachtsman (first African American to sail around the world solo via Cape of Good Hope and Cape Horn), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 2023)
Murray Gell-Mann, American physicist who predicted quarks (1969 Nobel Prize for Physics for theory of elementary particles), born in Manhattan, New York (d. 2019)
Launch of Zond 5, 1st lunar fly-around with Earth reentry
During Korean conflict, UN forces land at Inchon in South Korea
Probable Test flight for a manned fly-around (scooped by Apollo 8)
The Beatles headline The Great Prop Prom at Royal Albert Hall, London;The Rolling Stonesare one of the opening acts
Muhammad AlibeatsLeon Spinksin 15 rounds for heavyweight boxing title
Yuri Norstein, Russian animator (Hedgehog in the Fog), born in Andreyevka, Russia
Ryoko Kikuchi, Japanese cosmonaut (Soyuz TM-11) and reporter, born in Zama City, Kanagawa, Japan
"Lone Ranger" premieres on ABC-TV. Hi-yo, Silver! Away!
Carlo Rambaldi, Italian special effects artist (Alien, E.T.), born in Vigarano Mainarda, Italy (d. 2012)

The Beatles play at Public Auditorium in Cleveland, Ohio; police stop show during third song as some of the crowd climbed on the stage, after a 10 minute delay, and warning audience to stay seated, the concert resumed with the.
US TV series "Lost in Space" premieres
The John Bull becomes the oldest operable steam locomotive in the world when the Smithsonian Institution operates it under its own power outside Washington, D.C.
Edward Bouchet, American physicist, 1st African American to receive US Ph.D, born in New Haven, Connecticut (d. 1918)
Jan Matzeliger, Surinamese-Dutch-American inventor (shoe lasting machine), born in Paramaribo, Dutch Guyana (now Suriname) (d. 1889)
Bob Anderson, English fencer and renowned film fight choreographer (Star Wars, Lord of the Rings), born in Gosport, England (d. 2012)
Edna Adan Ismail, Somali activist and the first qualified-nurse midwife in Somaliland, born in Hargeisa, British Somaliland
First non-aristocratic, free public school in Europe is opened in Frascati, Italy
MLB Pittsburgh Pirates outfielders Llod Waner and PAul Waner become first and only brothers hit back-to-back homeruns
Soyuz 22 carries 2 cosmonauts into Earth orbit for 8 days
Jazz piano prodigy Margaret Johnson (20) makes her only recording, four sides withBillie Holidayin NYC: Johnson dies of tuberculosis less than a year later
MLB St. Louis CardinalsSteve Carltonsets record by striking out 19 NY Mets in a game
Norma MacMillan, Canadian voice actress (Casper the Ghost), born in Vancouver (d. 2001)
The Peach War: Munsee attack Dutch colony of New Amsterdam, killing about 40 colonists, possibly in retaliation for the murder of a Munsee woman picking peaches
Act of Independence of Central America: Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras and Nicaragua declare their independence from the Spanish Empire
Boston Red Sox pitcher "Smoky" Joe Wood ties then MLB record of 16 straight wins with a 2-1 victory over St. Louis Browns at Sportsman's Park
Napoleon Bonaparteand his French army reach the Kremlin in Moscow, where they watch the flames of the Great Fire of Moscow spread and grow
The locomotive "John Bull" operates for the first time in New Jersey on the Camden and Amboy Railroad
Pim Mulier forms Royal Haarlemsche Football Club based in Haarlem, Netherlands; oldest existing club in Dutch football
Soviet troops free Sofia, Bulgaria
First four-engined jet-propelled fighter plane tested, Columbus, Ohio
Gemini XI (Conrad/Gordon) returns to Earth
Donald Bailey, British civil engineer, invented the Bailey bridge, born in Rotherham, England (d. 1985)
Penny Singleton, American actress (Blondie) and voice actress (Jane-Jetsons), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 2003)
Wally Halder, Canadian ice hockey forward (Olympic gold 1948, tournament top scorer), born in Toronto, Ontario (d. 1994)
Julian "Cannonball" Adderley, American jazz alto saxophonist and bandleader of the 1950-60s hard bop era ("The Black Messiah"), born in Tampa, Florida (d. 1975)
George Washington, on authorization of Congress, appointsCasimir Pulaskias brigadier general in Continental Army cavalry
San Lorenzo del Escorial Palace in Madrid finished
Battle of Sekigahara, rise of the Tokugawa shogunate in Japan
Germantown in Pennsylvania is founded by 13 immigrant families
Antoinette Blackwell is the first US woman to be ordained a minister
Duke of WellingtonopensGeorge Stephenson's Liverpool and Manchester Railway
Maurice Richardannounces his retirement. He finishes his career with 544 goals, an NHL record at the time.
Volt/Stax records releasesOtis Redding's third studio album "Otis Blue - Otis Redding Sings Soul" in the US, Atlantic releases it in the UK; frequently ranked on top album lists

Jean-Sylvain Bailly, French astronomer noted for his computation of an orbit for Halley’s Comet (1759) and author of History of Ancient Astronomy from Its Origin to the Establishment of the School of Alexandria, born in Paris (d. 1793)
Aleksandr Mikhailovich Butlerov, Russian chemist who discovered hexamine in 1859, born in Chistopol, Kazan Governorate, Russian Empire (d. 1886)
M. Visvesvaraya, Indian civil engineer and statesman (19th Diwan of the Mysore Kingdom), born in Muddenahalli, Chikkaballapura, Kingdom of Mysore, born in (d. 1962)
Oskar Klein, Swedish theoretical physicist known for Kaluza–Klein theory, born in Mörby, Sweden (d. 1977)
Ismail Yasseen, Egyptian comedian and actor (Isamil Yassine as Tarzan, Ibn Hamidu), born in Suez, Egypt (d. 1972)
Jackie Cooper, American actor and director (Skippy, Superman), born in Los Angeles, California (d. 2011)
Patsy O. Sherman, American chemist and co-inventor of Scotchgard, born in Minneapolis, Minnesota (d. 2008)[1]
Sylvia Moy, American songwriter ("My Cherie Amour"; "This Old Heart Of Mine"; "It Takes Two"), born in Detroit, Michigan (d. 2017)
Merlin Olsen, American College-Pro Football HOF defensive tackle (Utah State Uni; First-team All-Pro 1966–70; 14 × Pro Bowl; Los Angeles Rams) and actor (Father Murphy), born in Logan, Utah (d. 2010)
